name: Vladimir Horowitz
abstract: Vladimir Samoylovich Horowitz (October 1 [O.S. September 18] 1903 – November 5, 1989) was a Russian and American pianist.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Charlap
about_quotes.evidence: I know the first record I bought was Vladimir Horowitz playing Liszt... That’s some incredible piano playing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Charlap interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: John Malachi
about_quotes.evidence: Of course, I had heard of him and... I had never heard him play in person then, but I knew who he was

about_quotes.speaker: Rossano Sportiello
about_quotes.evidence: I wish like there would be someone that could play classical as masterfully as Vladimir Horowitz
about_quotes.doc_title: Rossano Sportiello interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, October 2, 2015 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: when Vladimir Horowitz comes out to play... they don’t go to the microphone and say, I hope you like this little tune by Schubert
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Shelly Berg
about_quotes.evidence: Vladimir Horowitz came out of one of his retirements and played in Houston where I was living... I left the Vladimir Horowitz concert so enthused about practicing and so excited.
about_quotes.doc_title: Shelly Berg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 15, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Dorothy Donegan
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Sherman wanted to put me in a concert opposite Vladimir Horowitz. And so I outsold Horowitz.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dorothy Donegan — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
